text: Let his roots be dried up beneath, and his harvest destroyed above.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let the memory of him perish from the earth, and let not his name be renowned in the streets.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall drive him out of light into darkness, and shall remove him out of the world.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His seed shall not subsist, nor his offspring among his people, nor any remnants in his country.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They that come after him shall be astonished at his day, and horror shall fall upon them that went before.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These then are the tabernacles of the wicked, and this the place of him that knoweth not God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Job answered, and said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: How long do you afflict my soul, and break me in pieces with words?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold, these ten times you confound me, and are not ashamed to oppress me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For if I have been ignorant, my ignorance shall be with me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But you set yourselves up against me, and reprove me with my reproaches.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At least now understand, that God hath not afflicted me with an equal judgment, and compassed me with his scourges.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold I shall cry suffering violence, and no one will hear: I shall cry aloud, and there is none to judge.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath hedged in my path round about, and I cannot pass, and in my way he hath set darkness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath stripped me of my glory, and hath taken the crown from my head.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath destroyed me on every side, and I am lost, and he hath taken away my hope, as from a tree that is plucked up.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His wrath is kindled against me, and he hath counted me as his enemy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His troops have come together, and have made themselves a way by me, and have besieged my tabernacle round about.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath put my brethren far from me, and my acquaintance like strangers have departed from me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My kinsmen have forsaken me, and they that knew me, have forgotten me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They that dwell in my house, and my maidservants have counted me as a stranger, and I have been like an alien in their eyes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I called my servant, and he gave me no answer, I entreated him with my own mouth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My wife hath abhorred my breath, and I entreated the children of my womb.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Even fools despised me, and when I was gone from them, they spoke against me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They that were sometime my counsellors, have abhorred me: and he whom I loved most is turned against me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The flesh being consumed, my bone hath cleaved to my skin, and nothing but lips are left about my teeth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Have pity on me, have pity on me, at least you my friends, because the hand of the Lord hath touched me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Why do you persecute me as God, and glut yourselves with my flesh?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who will grant me that my words may be written? who will grant me that they may be marked down in a book?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: With an iron pen and in a plate of lead, or else be graven with an instrument in flint stone?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I know that my Redeemer liveth, and in the last day I shall rise out of the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I shall be clothed again with my skin, and in my flesh I shall see my God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whom I myself shall see, and my eyes shall behold, and not another: this my hope is laid up in my bosom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Why then do you say now: Let us persecute him, and let us find occasion of word against him?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Flee then from the face of the sword, for the sword is the revenger of iniquities: and know ye that there is a judgment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Sophar the Naamathite answered, and said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore various thoughts succeed one another in me, and my mind is hurried away to different things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The doctrine with which thou reprovest me, I will hear, and the spirit of my understanding shall answer for me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This I know from the beginning, since man was placed upon the earth,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That the praise of the wicked is short, and the joy of the hypocrite but for a moment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If his pride mount up even to heaven, and his head touch the clouds:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the end he shall be destroyed like a dunghill, and they that had seen him, shall say: Where is he?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As a dream that fleeth away he shall not be found, he shall pass as a vision of the night:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The eyes that had seen him, shall see him no more, neither shall his place any more behold him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His children shall be oppressed with want, and his hands shall render to him his sorrow.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His bones shall be filled with the vices of his youth, and they shall sleep with him in the dust.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For when evil shall be sweet in his mouth, he will hide it under his tongue.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He will spare it, and not leave it, and will hide it in his throat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His bread in his belly shall be turned into the gall of asps within him,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The riches which he hath swallowed, he shall vomit up, and God shall draw them out of his belly.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall suck the head of asps, and the viper’s tongue shall kill him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let him not see the streams of the river, the brooks of honey and of butter.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall be punished for all that he did, and yet shall not be consumed: according to the multitude of his devices so also shall he suffer.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because he broke in and stripped the poor: he hath violently taken away a house which he did not build.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And yet his belly was not filled: and when he hath the things he coveted, he shall not be able to possess them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There was nothing left of his meat, and therefore nothing shall continue of his goods:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When he shall be filled, he shall be straitened, he shall burn, and every sorrow shall fall upon him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: May his belly be filled, that God may send forth the wrath of his indignation upon him, and rain down his war upon him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall flee from weapons of iron, and shall fall upon a bow of brass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sword is drawn out, and cometh forth from its scabbard, and glittereth in his bitterness: the terrible ones shall go and come upon him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All darkness is hid in his secret places: a fire that is not kindled shall devour him, he shall be afflicted when left in his tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The heavens shall reveal his iniquity, and the earth shall rise up against him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The offspring of his house shall be exposed, he shall be pulled down in the day of God’s wrath.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the portion of a wicked man from God, and the inheritance of his doings from the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Job answered, and said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hear, I beseech you, my words, and do penance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Suffer me, and I will speak, and after, if you please, laugh at my words.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Is my debate against man, that I should not have just reason to be troubled?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hearken to me and be astonished, and lay your finger on your mouth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As for me, when I remember, I am afraid, and trembling taketh hold on my flesh.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Why then do the wicked live, are they advanced, and strengthened with riches?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their seed continueth before them, a multitude of kinsmen, and of children’s children in their sight.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their houses are secure and peaceable, and the rod of God is not upon them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their cattle have conceived, and failed not: their cow has calved, and is not deprived of her fruit.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their little ones go out like a flock, and their children dance and play.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They take the timbrel, and the harp, and rejoice at the sound of the organ.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They spend their days in wealth, and in a moment they go down to hell.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who have said to God: Depart from us, we desire not the knowledge of thy ways.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who is the Almighty, that we should serve him? and what doth it profit us if we pray to him?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Yet because their good things are not in their hand, may the counsel of the wicked be far from me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: How often shall the lamp of the wicked be put out, and a deluge come upon them, and he shall distribute the sorrows of his wrath?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall be as chaff before the face of the wind, and as ashes which the whirlwind scattereth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: God shall lay up the sorrow of the father for his children: and when he shall repay, then shall he know.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His eyes shall see his own destruction, and he shall drink of the wrath of the Almighty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For what is it to him what befalleth his house after him: and if the number of his months be diminished by one half?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shall any one teach God knowledge, who judgeth those that are high?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: One man dieth strong, and hale, rich and happy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His bowels are full of fat, and his bones are moistened with marrow.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But another dieth in bitterness of soul without any riches:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And yet they shall sleep together in the dust, and worms shall cover them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Surely I know your thoughts, and your unjust judgments against me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For you say: Where is the house of the prince? and where are the dwelling places of the wicked?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Ask any one of them that go by the way, and you shall perceive that he knoweth these same things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because the wicked man is reserved to the day of destruction, and he shall be brought to the day of wrath.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who shall reprove his way to his face? and who shall repay him what he hath done?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall be brought to the graves, and shall watch in the heap of the dead.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath been acceptable to the gravel of Cocytus, and he shall draw every man after him, and there are innumerable before him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: How then do ye comfort me in vain, whereas your answer is shewn to be repugnant to truth?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Eliphaz the Themanite answered, and said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Can man be compared with God, even though he were of perfect knowledge?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What doth it profit God if thou be just? or what dost thou give him if thy way be unspotted?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shall he reprove thee for fear, and come with thee into judgment:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And not for thy manifold wickedness and thy infinite iniquities?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thou hast taken away the pledge of thy brethren without cause, and stripped the naked of their clothing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou hast not given water to the weary, thou hast withdrawn bread from the hungry.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the strength of thy arm thou didst possess the land, and being the most mighty thou holdest it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou hast sent widows away empty, and the arms of the fatherless thou hast broken in pieces.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore art thou surrounded with snares, and sudden fear troubleth thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And didst thou think that thou shouldst not see darkness, and that thou shouldst not be covered with the violence of overflowing waters?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Dost not thou think that God is higher than heaven, and is elevated above the height of the stars?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou sayst: What doth God know? and he judgeth as it were through a mist.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The clouds are his covert, and he doth not consider our things, and he walketh about the poles of heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Dost thou desire to keep the path of ages, which wicked men have trodden?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who were taken away before their time, and a flood hath overthrown their foundation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who said to God: Depart from us: and looked upon the Almighty as if he could do nothing:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whereas he had filled their houses with good things: whose way of thinking be far from me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The just shall see, and shall rejoice, and the innocent shall laugh them to scorn.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Is not their exaltation cut down, and hath not fire devoured the remnants of them?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Submit thyself then to him, and be at peace: and thereby thou shalt have the best fruits.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Receive the law of his mouth, and lay up his words in thy heart.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If thou wilt return to the Almighty, thou shalt be built up, and shalt put away iniquity far from thy tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall give for earth flint, and for flint torrents of gold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Almighty shall be against thy enemies, and silver shall be heaped together for thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then shalt thou abound in delights in the Almighty, and shalt lift up thy face to God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt pray to him, and he will hear thee, and thou shalt pay vows.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt decree a thing, and it shall come to thee, and light shall shine in thy ways.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he that hath been humbled, shall be in glory: and he that shall bow down his eyes, he shall be saved.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The innocent shall be saved, and he shall be saved by the cleanness of his hands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Job answered, and said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now also my words are in bitterness, and the hand of my scourge is more grievous than my mourning.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who will grant me that I might know and find him, and come even to his throne?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I would set judgment before him, and would fill my mouth with complaints.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That I might know the words that he would answer me, and understand what he would say to me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I would not that he should contend with me with much strength, nor overwhelm me with the weight of his greatness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let him propose equity against me, and let my judgment come to victory.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if I go to the east, he appeareth not; if to the west, I shall not understand him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If to the left hand, what shall I do? I shall not take hold on him: if I turn myself to the right hand, I shall not see him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he knoweth my way, and has tried me as gold that passeth through the fire:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My foot hath followed his steps, I have kept his way, and have not declined from it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have not departed from the commandments of his lips, and the words of his mouth I have hid in my bosom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he is alone, and no man can turn away his thought: and whatsoever his soul hath desired, that hath he done.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he shall have fulfilled his will in me, many other like things are also at hand with him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And therefore I am troubled at his presence, and when I consider him I am made pensive with fear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: God hath softened my heart, and the Almighty hath troubled me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I have not perished because of the darkness that hangs over me, neither hath the mist covered my face.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Times are not hid from the Almighty: but they that know him, know not his days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Some have removed landmarks, have taken away flocks by force, and fed them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They have driven away the ass of the fatherless, and have taken away the widow’s ox for a pledge.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They have overturned the way of the poor, and have oppressed together the meek of the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Others like wild asses in the desert go forth to their work: by watching for a prey they get bread for their children.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They reap the field that is not their own, and gather the vintage of his vineyard whom by violence they have oppressed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They send men away naked, taking away their clothes who have no covering in the cold:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who are wet, with the showers of the mountains, and having no covering embrace the stones.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They have violently robbed the fatherless, and stripped the poor common people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From the naked and them that go without clothing, and from the hungry they have taken away the ears of corn.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They have taken their rest at noon among the stores of them, who after having trodden the winepresses suffer thirst.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Out of the cities they have made men to groan, and the soul of the wounded hath cried out, and God doth not suffer it to pass unrevenged.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They have been rebellious to the light, they have not known his ways, neither have they returned by his paths.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The murderer riseth at the very break of day, he killeth the needy, and the poor man: but in the night he will be as a thief.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The eye of the adulterer observeth darkness, saying: No eye shall see me: and he will cover his face.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He diggeth through houses in the dark, as in the day they had appointed for themselves, and they have not known the light.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If the morning suddenly appear, it is to them the shadow of death: and they walk in darkness as if it were in light.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He is light upon the face of the water: cursed be his portion on the earth, let him not walk by the way of the vineyards.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let him pass from the snow waters to excessive heat, and his sin even to hell.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let mercy forget him: may worms be his sweetness: let him be remembered no more, but be broken in pieces as an unfruitful tree.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he hath fed the barren that beareth not, and to the widow he hath done no good.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath pulled down the strong by his might: and when he standeth up, he shall not trust to his life.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: God hath given him place for penance, and he abuseth it unto pride: but his eyes are upon his ways.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They are lifted up for a little while and shall not stand, and shall be brought down as all things, and shall be taken away, and as the tops of the ears of corn they shall be broken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if it be not so, who can convince me that I have lied, and set my words before God?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Baldad the Suhite answered, and I said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Power and terror are with him, who maketh peace in his high places.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Is there any numbering of his soldiers? and upon whom shall not his light arise?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Can man be justified compared with God, or he that is born of a woman appear clean?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold even the moon doth not shine, and the stars are not pure in his sight.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: How much less man that is rottenness and the son of man who is a worm?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Job answered, and said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whose helper art thou? is it of him that is weak? and dost thou hold up the arm of him that has no strength?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To whom hast thou given counsel? perhaps to him that hath no wisdom, and thou hast shewn thy very great prudence.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whom hast thou desired to teach? was it not him that made life?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold the giants groan under the waters, and they that dwell with them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hell is naked before him, and there is no covering for destruction.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He stretched out the north over the empty space, and hangeth the earth upon nothing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He bindeth up the waters in his clouds, so that they break not out and fall down together.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He withholdeth the face of his throne, and spreadeth his cloud over it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath set bounds about the waters, till light and darkness come to an end.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The pillars of heaven tremble, and dread at his beck.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: By his power the seas are suddenly gathered together, and his wisdom has struck the proud one.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His spirit hath adorned the heavens, and his obstetric hand brought forth the winding serpent.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lo, these things are said in part of his ways: and seeing we have heard scarce a little drop of his word, who shall be able to behold the thunder of his greatness?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Job also added, taking up his parable, and said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As God liveth, who hath taken away my judgment, and the Almighty, who hath brought my soul to bitterness,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As long as breath remaineth in me, and the spirit of God in my nostrils,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My lips shall not speak iniquity, neither shall my tongue contrive lying.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: God forbid that I should judge you to be just: till I die I will not depart from my innocence.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My justification, which I have begun to hold, I will not forsake: for my heart doth not reprehend me in all my life.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let my enemy be as the ungodly, and my adversary as the wicked one.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For what is the hope of the hypocrite if through covetousness he take by violence, and God deliver not his soul?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Will God hear his cry, when distress shall come upon him?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Or can he delight himself in the Almighty, and call upon God at all times?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will teach you by the hand of God, what the Almighty hath, and I will not conceal it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold you all know it, and why do you speak vain things without cause?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the portion of a wicked man with God, and the inheritance of the violent, which they shall receive of the Almighty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If his sons be multiplied, they shall be for the sword, and his grandsons shall not be filled with bread.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They that shall remain of him, shall be buried in death, and his widows shall not weep.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If he shall heap together silver as earth, and prepare raiment as clay,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall prepare indeed, but the just man shall be clothed with it: and the innocent shall divide the silver.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath built his house as a moth, and as a keeper he hath made a booth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The rich man when he shall sleep shall take away nothing with him: he shall open his eyes and find nothing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Poverty like water shall take hold on him, a tempest shall oppress him in the night:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A burning wind shall take him up, and carry him away, and as a whirlwind shall snatch him from his place.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall cast upon him, and shall not spare: out of his hand he would willingly flee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall clasp his hands upon him, and shall hiss at him, beholding his place.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Silver hath beginnings of its veins, and gold hath a place wherein it is melted.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Iron is taken out of the earth, and stone melted with heat is turned into brass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath set a time for darkness, and the end of all things he considereth, the stone also that is in the dark and the shadow of death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The flood divideth from the people that are on their journey, those whom the food of the needy man hath forgotten, and who cannot be come at.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The land, out of which bread grew in its place, hath been overturned with fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The stones of it are the place of sapphires, and the clods of it are gold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The bird hath not known the path, neither hath the eye of the vulture beheld it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The children of the merchants have not trodden it, neither hath the lioness passed by it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath stretched forth his hand to the flint, he hath overturned mountains from the roots.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the rocks he hath cut out rivers, and his eye hath seen every precious thing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The depths also of rivers he hath searched, and hidden things he hath brought forth to light.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But where is wisdom to be found, and where is the place of understanding?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Man knoweth not the price thereof, neither is it found in the land of them that live in delights.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The depth saith: It is not in me: and the sea saith: It is not with me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The finest gold shall not purchase it, neither shall silver be weighed in exchange for it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It shall not be compared with the dyed colours of India, or with the most precious stone sardonyx, or the sapphire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Gold or crystal cannot equal it, neither shall any vessels of gold be changed for it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: High and eminent things shall not be mentioned in comparison of it: but wisdom is drawn out of secret places.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The topaz of Ethiopia shall not be equal to it, neither shall it be compared to the cleanest dyeing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whence then cometh wisdom? and where is the place of understanding?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It is hid from the eyes of all living, and the fowls of the air know it not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Destruction and death have said: With our ears we have heard the fame thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: God understandeth the way of it, and he knoweth the place thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he beholdeth the ends of the world: and looketh on all things that are under heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who made a weight for the winds, and weighed the waters by measure.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When he gave a law for the rain, and a way for the sounding storms.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then he saw it, and declared, and prepared, and searched it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to man: Behold the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom: and to depart from evil, is understanding.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Job also added, taking up his parable, and said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who will grant me, that I might be according to the months past, according to the days in which God kept me?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When his lamp shined over my head, and I walked by his light in darkness?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As I was in the days of my youth, when God was secretly in my tabernacle?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When the Almighty was with me: and my servants round about me?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When I washed my feet with butter, and the rock poured me out rivers of oil?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When I went out to the gate of the city, and in the street they prepared me a chair?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The young men saw me, and hid themselves: and the old men rose up and stood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The princes ceased to speak, and laid the finger on their mouth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The rulers held their peace, and their tongue cleaved to their throat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The ear that heard me blessed me, and the eye that saw me gave witness to me:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because I had delivered the poor man that cried out; and the fatherless, that had no helper.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The blessing of him that was ready to perish came upon me, and I comforted the heart of the widow.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I was clad with justice: and I clothed myself with my judgment, as with a robe and a diadem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I was an eye to the blind, and a foot to the lame.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I was the father of the poor: and the cause which I knew not, I searched out most diligently.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I broke the jaws of the wicked man, and out of his teeth I took away the prey.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I said: I shall die in my nest, and as a palm tree shall multiply my days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My root is opened beside the waters, and dew shall continue in my harvest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My glory shall always be renewed, and my bow in my hand shall be repaired.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They that heard me, waited for my sentence, and being attentive held their peace at my counsel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To my words they durst add nothing, and my speech dropped upon them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They waited for me as for rain, and they opened their mouth as for a latter shower.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If at any time I laughed on them, they believed not, and the light of my countenance fell not on earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If I had a mind to go to them, I sat first, and when I sat as a king, with his army standing about him, yet I was a comforter of them that mourned.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But now the younger in time scorn me, whose fathers I would not have set with the dogs of my flock:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The strength of whose hands was to me as nothing, and they were thought unworthy of life itself.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Barren with want and hunger, who gnawed in the wilderness, disfigured with calamity and misery.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they ate grass, and barks of trees, and the root of junipers was their food.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who snatched up these things out of the valleys, and when they had found any of them, they ran to them with a cry.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They dwelt in the desert places of torrents, and in caves of earth, or upon the gravel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They pleased themselves among these kind of things, and counted it delightful to be under the briers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The children of foolish and base men, and not appearing at all upon the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now I am turned into their song, and am become their byword.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They abhor me, and flee far from me, and are not afraid to spit in my face.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he hath opened his quiver, and hath afflicted me, and hath put a bridle into my mouth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At the right hand of my rising, my calamities forthwith arose: they have overthrown my feet, and have overwhelmed me with their paths as with waves.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They have destroyed my ways, they have lain in wait against me, and they have prevailed, and there was none to help.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They have rushed in upon me, as when a wall is broken, and a gate opened, and have rolled themselves down to my miseries.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I am brought to nothing: as a wind thou hast taken away my desire: and my prosperity hath passed away like a cloud.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now my soul fadeth within myself, and the days of affliction possess me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the night my bone is pierced with sorrows: and they that feed upon me, do not sleep.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: With the multitude of them my garment is consumed, and they have girded me about, as with the collar of my coat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I am compared to dirt, and am likened to embers and ashes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I cry to thee, and thou hearest me not: I stand up, and thou dost not regard me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou art changed to be cruel toward me, and in the hardness of thy hand thou art against me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou hast lifted me up, and set me as it were upon the wind, and thou hast mightily dashed me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I know that thou wilt deliver me to death, where a house is appointed for every one that liveth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But yet thou stretchest not forth thy hand to their consumption: and if they shall fall down thou wilt save.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I wept heretofore for him that was afflicted, and my soul had compassion on the poor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I expected good things, and evils are come upon me: I waited for light, and darkness broke out.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My inner parts have boiled without any rest, the days of affliction have prevented me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I went mourning without indignation; I rose up, and cried in the crowd.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I was the brother of dragons, and companion of ostriches.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My skin is become black upon me, and my bones are dried up with heat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My harp is turned to mourning, and my organ into the voice of those that weep.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I made a covenant with my eyes, that I would not so much as think upon a virgin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For what part should God from above have in me, and what inheritance the Almighty from on high?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Is not destruction to the wicked, and aversion to them that work iniquity?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Doth not he consider my ways, and number all my steps?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If I have walked in vanity, and my foot hath made haste to deceit:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let him weigh me in a just balance, and let God know my simplicity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If my step hath turned out of the way, and if my heart hath followed my eyes, and if a spot hath cleaved to my hands:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then let me sow and let another reap: and let my offspring be rooted out.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If my heart hath been deceived upon a woman, and if I have laid wait at my friend’s door:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let my wife be the harlot of another, and let other men lie with her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For this is a heinous crime, and a most grievous iniquity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It is a fire that devoureth even to destruction, and rooteth up all things that spring.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If I have despised to abide judgment with my manservant, or my maidservant, when they had any controversy against me:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For what shall I do when God shall rise to judge? and when he shall examine, what shall I answer him?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Did not he that made me in the womb make him also: and did not one and the same form me in the womb?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If I have denied to the poor what they desired, and have made the eyes of the widow wait:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If I have eaten my morsel alone, and the fatherless hath not eaten thereof:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: (For from my infancy mercy grew up with me: and it came out with me from my mother’s womb:)

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If I have despised him that was perishing for want of clothing, and the poor man that had no covering:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If his sides have not blessed me, and if he were not warmed with the fleece of my sheep:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If I have lifted up my hand against the fatherless, even when I saw myself superior in the gate:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let my shoulder fall from its joint, and let my arm with its bones be broken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I have always feared God as waves swelling over me, and his weight I was unable to bear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If I have thought gold my strength, and have said to fine gold: My confidence:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If I have rejoiced over my great riches, and because my hand had gotten much.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If I beheld the sun when it shined and the moon going in brightness:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And my heart in secret hath rejoiced, and I have kissed my hand with, my mouth:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which is a very great iniquity, and a denial against the most high God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If I have been glad at the downfall of him that hated me, and have rejoiced that evil had found him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I have not given my mouth to sin, by wishing a curse to his soul.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If the men of my tabernacle have not said: Who will give us of his flesh that we may be filled?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The stranger did not stay without, my door was open to the traveller.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If as a man I have hid my sin, and have concealed my iniquity in my bosom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If I have been afraid at a very great multitude, and the contempt of kinsmen hath terrified me: and have not rather held my peace, and not gone out of the door.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who would grant me a hearing, that the Almighty may hear my desire: and that he himself that judgeth would write a book,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That I may carry it on my shoulder, and put it about me as a crown?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At every step of mine I would pronounce it, and offer it as to a prince.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If my land cry against me, and with it the furrows thereof mourn:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If I have eaten the fruits thereof without money, and have afflicted the soul of the tillers thereof:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let thistles grow up to me instead of wheat, and thorns instead of barley.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So these three men ceased to answer Job, because he seemed just to himself.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Eliu the son of Barachel the Buzite of the kindred of Ram, was angry and was moved to indignation: now he was angry against Job, because he said he was just before God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he was angry with his friends, because they had not found a reasonable answer, but only had condemned Job.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So Eliu waited while Job was speaking because they were his elders that were speaking.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But when he saw that the three were not able to answer, he was exceedingly angry.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Eliu the son of Barachel the Buzite answered, and said: I am younger in days, and you are more ancient, therefore hanging down my head, I was afraid to shew you my opinion.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I hoped that greater age would speak, and that a multitude of years would teach wisdom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But, as I see, there is a spirit in men, and the inspiration of the Almighty giveth understanding.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They that are aged are not the wise men, neither do the ancients understand judgment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore I will speak: Hearken to me, I also will shew you my wisdom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I have waited for your words, I have given ear to your wisdom, as long as you were disputing in words.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as long as I thought you said some thing, I considered: but, as I see, there is none of you that can convince Job, and answer his words.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lest you should say: We have found wisdom, God hath cast him down, not man.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath spoken nothing to me, and I will not answer him according to your words.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They were afraid, and answered no more, and they left off speaking.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore because I have waited, and they have not spoken: they stood, and answered no more:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I also will answer my part, and will shew my knowledge.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I am full of matter to speak of, and the spirit of my bowels straiteneth me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold, my belly is as new wine which wanteth vent, which bursteth the new vessels.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will speak and take breath a little: I will open my lips, and will answer.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will not accept the person of man, and I will not level God with man.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I know not how long I shall continue, and whether after a while my Maker may take me away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hear therefore, O Job, my speeches, and hearken to all my words.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold now I have opened my mouth, let my tongue speak within my jaws.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My words are from my upright heart, and my lips shall speak a pure sentence.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The spirit of God made me, and the breath of the Almighty gave me life.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If thou canst, answer me, and stand up against my face.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold God hath made me as well as thee, and of the same clay I also was formed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But yet let not my wonder terrify thee, and let not my eloquence be burdensome to thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now thou hast said in my hearing, and I have heard the voice of thy words:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I am clean, and without sin: I am unspotted, and there is no iniquity in me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because he hath found complaints against me, therefore he hath counted me for his enemy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath put my feet in the stocks, he hath observed all my paths.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now this is the thing in which thou art not justified: I will answer thee, that God is greater than man.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Dost thou strive against him, because he hath not answered thee to all words?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: God speaketh once, and repeateth not the selfsame thing the second time.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: By a dream in a vision by night, when deep sleep falleth upon men, and they are sleeping in their beds:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then he openeth the ears of men, and teaching instructeth them in what they are to learn.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That he may withdraw a man from the things he is doing, and may deliver him from pride.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Rescuing his soul from corruption: and his life from passing to the sword.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He rebuketh also by sorrow in the bed, and he maketh all his bones to wither.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Bread becometh abominable to him in his life, and to his soul the meat which before he desired.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His flesh shall be consumed away, and his bones that were covered shall be made bare.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His soul hath drawn near to corruption, and his life to the destroyers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If there shall be an angel speaking for him, one among thousands, to declare man’s uprightness,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall have mercy on him, and shall say: Deliver him, that he may not go down to corruption: I have found wherein I may be merciful to him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His flesh is consumed with punishments, let him return to the days of his youth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall pray to God, and he will be gracious to him: and he shall see his face with joy, and he will render to man his justice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall look upon men, and shall say: I have sinned, and indeed I have offended, and I have not received what I have deserved.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath delivered his soul from going into destruction, that it may live and see the light.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold, all these things God worketh three times within every one.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That he may withdraw their souls from corruption, and enlighten them with the light of the living.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Attend, Job, and hearken to me, and hold thy peace, whilst I speak.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if thou hast any thing to say, answer me, speak: for I would have thee to appear just.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if thou have not, hear me: hold thy peace, and I will teach thee wisdom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Eliu continued his discourse, and said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hear ye, wise men, my words, and ye learned, hearken to me:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the ear trieth words, and the mouth discerneth meats by the taste.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let us choose to us judgment, and let us see among ourselves what is the best.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For Job hath said: I am just, and God hath overthrown my judgment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For in judging me there is a lie: my arrow is violent without any sin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What man is there like Job, who drinketh up scorning like water?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who goeth in company with them that work iniquity, and walketh with wicked men?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he hath said: Man shall not please God, although he run with him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore, ye men of understanding, hear me: far from God be wickedness, and iniquity from the Almighty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he will render to a man his work, and according to the ways of every one he will reward them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For in very deed God will not condemn without cause, neither will the Almighty pervert judgment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What other hath he appointed over the earth? or whom hath he set over the world which he made?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If he turn his heart to him, he shall draw his spirit and breath unto himself.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All flesh shall perish together, and man shall return into ashes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If then thou hast understanding, hear what is said, and hearken to the voice of my words.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Can he be healed that loveth not judgment? and how dost thou so far condemn him that is just?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who saith to the king: Thou art an apostate: who calleth rulers ungodly:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who accepteth not the persons of princes: nor hath regarded the tyrant, when he contended against the poor man: for all are the work of his hands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall suddenly die, and the people shall be troubled at midnight, and they shall pass, and take away the violent without hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For his eyes are upon the ways of men, and he considereth all their steps.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There is no darkness, and there is no shadow of death, where they may be hid who work iniquity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For it is no longer in the power of man to enter into judgment with God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall break in pieces many and innumerable, and shall make others to stand in their stead.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he knoweth their works: and therefore he shall bring night on them, and they shall be destroyed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath struck them, as being wicked, in open sight.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who as it were on purpose have revolted from him, and would not understand all his ways:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So that they caused the cry of the needy to come to him, and he heard the voice of the poor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For when he granteth peace, who is there that can condemn? When he hideth his countenance, who is there that can behold him, whether it regard nations, or all men?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who maketh a man that is a hypocrite to reign for the sins of the people?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Seeing then I have spoken of God, I will not hinder thee in thy turn.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If I have erred, teach thou me: if I have spoken iniquity, I will add no more.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Doth God require it of thee, because it hath displeased thee? for thou begannest to speak, and not I: but if thou know any thing better, speak.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let men of understanding speak to me, and let a wise man hearken to me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But Job hath spoken foolishly, and his words sound not discipline.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My father, let Job be tried even to the end: cease not from the man of iniquity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because he addeth blasphemy upon his sins, let him be tied fast in the mean time amongst us: and then let him provoke God to judgment with his speeches.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moreover Eliu spoke these words:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Doth thy thought seem right to thee, that thou shouldst say: I am more just than God?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thou saidst: That which is right doth not please thee: or what will it profit thee if I sin?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore I will answer thy words, and thy friends with thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
